data.content:
DETR
DETR refers to end-to-end Transformer-based object detection models that eliminate the need for Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).<SEP>DETR is an end-to-end object detection model based on the Transformer architecture, which RT-DETR improves upon.<SEP>DETR is an end-to-end object detection model based on the Transformer architecture.<SEP>DETR is a Transformer-based object detection model that eliminates the need for Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).

YOLO
YOLO is a series of real-time object detection frameworks known for their balance between speed and accuracy.<SEP>YOLO is a series of real-time object detection models known for their speed and simplicity.

RT-DETR
RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to outperform YOLOs in both speed and accuracy.<SEP>RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detector proposed in the text, designed to improve inference speed and accuracy.<SEP>RT-DETR is an object detection model represented by red circles in the scatter plot, labeled as "Ours".<SEP>RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to improve upon DETRs.

Non-Maximum Suppression
Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) is a post-processing step used in object detection to remove redundant detections.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression is a post-processing technique used in object detection to eliminate redundant detections.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression is a technique used to eliminate overlapping detections in object detection.

Peking University
Peking University is an educational institution in Shenzhen, China, associated with the research presented in the paper.<SEP>Peking University is an organization affiliated with some of the authors of the research paper.

Baidu Inc
Baidu Inc is an organization based in Beijing, China, involved in the research and development of object detection models.<SEP>Baidu Inc is an organization affiliated with some of the authors of the research paper.

COCO
COCO is a dataset used for evaluating object detection models, measuring performance in terms of Average Precision (AP).

Objects365
Objects365 is a large-scale dataset used for pre-training object detection models.<SEP>Objects365 is a large-scale dataset used for pre-training RT-DETR to improve its performance.

Yian Zhao
Yian Zhao is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Yian Zhao is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Wenyu Lv
Wenyu Lv is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Wenyu Lv is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Shangliang Xu
Shangliang Xu is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Guanzhong Wang
Guanzhong Wang is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Jinman Wei
Jinman Wei is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Qingqing Dang
Qingqing Dang is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Yi Liu
Yi Liu is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

Jie Chen
Jie Chen is one of the researchers who contributed to the development of RT-DETR.

CNN
CNN refers to Convolutional Neural Networks, which are commonly used in real-time object detection architectures.

Transformer
Transformer is a neural network architecture used in DETRs for object detection.

Object Detection
Object detection is the task of identifying and localizing objects within images or videos.

Real-Time Object Detection
Real-Time Object Detection refers to the ability of a model to detect objects quickly enough for real-time applications.

T4 GPU
T4 GPU is a graphics processing unit used for testing model performance.<SEP>T4 GPU is a type of graphics processing unit used for running and benchmarking object detection models.<SEP>T4 GPU is the hardware platform on which RT-DETR's performance is measured.<SEP>T4 GPU is a graphics processing unit used for testing and optimizing object detection algorithms.

DINO
DINO is another object detection model that RT-DETR outperforms in terms of accuracy and speed.<SEP>DINO is a variant of DETR that suggests mixed query selection for better initialization.

Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection
Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a technique proposed in the paper to improve the quality of initial queries in the decoder.<SEP>Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a technique introduced in RT-DETR to enhance decoder performance by optimizing query quality.

Hybrid Encoder
Hybrid Encoder is a component of RT-DETR designed to efficiently process multi-scale features.<SEP>Hybrid Encoder is an efficient design used in RT-DETR to replace the vanilla Transformer encoder, improving inference speed.

Real-Time DEtection TRansformer
Real-Time DEtection TRansformer is the full name of RT-DETR, representing a novel approach to real-time object detection.<SEP>Real-Time DEtection TRansformer (RT-DETR) is a real-time end-to-end object detection model proposed in the paper, designed to outperform YOLOs in both speed and accuracy.<SEP>Real-Time DEtection TRansformer is the full name of RT-DETR, an efficient object detection model.

Multi-Scale Features
Multi-Scale Features refer to features extracted at different scales to capture objects of varying sizes.

Transformer Encoder
Transformer Encoder is a component of the original DETR model that RT-DETR aims to improve upon.

YOLO Detectors
YOLO Detectors are a family of real-time object detection models that RT-DETR outperforms in both speed and accuracy.

COCO val2017
COCO val2017 is a benchmark dataset used for evaluating object detection models.<SEP>COCO val2017 is a benchmark dataset used to evaluate the performance of RT-DETR.<SEP>COCO val2017 is a dataset used for benchmarking object detection algorithms.

NMS
NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping output boxes by applying confidence and IoU thresholds.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression, a post-processing technique used in object detection to eliminate redundant detections.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping boxes, which RT-DETR eliminates.<SEP>NMS stands for Non-Maximum Suppression, a post-processing algorithm used to remove redundant detections in object detection.

YOLOv1
YOLOv1 is the first CNN-based one-stage object detector that achieved true real-time object detection.

Deformable-DETR
Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves training convergence with multi-scale features.<SEP>Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves efficiency through deformable attention mechanisms.

DAB-DETR
DAB-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through iterative refinement.<SEP>DAB-DETR is another variant of DETR that enhances performance with dynamic anchor boxes.

Group-DETR
Group-DETR is a variant of DETR that introduces group-wise one-to-many assignment.

DN-DETR
DN-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through denoising training.

Efficient DETR
Efficient DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost.

Sparse DETR
Sparse DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost by reducing encoder and decoder layers.

Lite DETR
Lite DETR is a variant of DETR that enhances encoder efficiency.

Conditional DETR
Conditional DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces query optimization difficulty.

Anchor DETR
Anchor DETR is a variant of DETR that decreases query optimization difficulty.

Figure 1
Figure 1 is a visual representation comparing the performance of RT-DETR with other models.<SEP>Figure 1 is a scatter plot chart that compares various object detection models based on accuracy and latency.<SEP>Figure 1 is referenced in the text to illustrate the performance results of RT-DETR models.

Confidence Threshold
Confidence threshold is a parameter used in NMS to filter out prediction boxes with scores below a specified value.<SEP>Confidence Threshold is a parameter used in NMS to filter output boxes.<SEP>Confidence Threshold is a parameter used in object detection algorithms that determines the minimum confidence level for detecting objects.

Figure 2
Figure 2 illustrates the number of boxes at different confidence thresholds.<SEP>Figure 2 is a bar chart comparing the performance of YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 under different confidence thresholds.

IoU Threshold
IoU threshold is a parameter used in NMS to determine when two boxes are considered overlapping, based on their intersection over union.<SEP>IoU Threshold is a parameter used in NMS to filter overlapping boxes.<SEP>IoU Threshold is a parameter used in object detection to measure the overlap between predicted and ground truth bounding boxes.

Anchor-Based
Anchor-Based refers to a category of YOLO detectors that use predefined anchor boxes for object detection.

Multi-Layer Decoder Architecture
The multi-layer decoder architecture in DETR allows RT-DETR to support flexible speed tuning without retraining.

Anchor-Free
Anchor-Free refers to a category of YOLO detectors that do not rely on predefined anchor boxes for object detection.

YOLOv5
YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model used for analysis in the text.<SEP>An anchor-based real-time object detection model developed by Ultralytics.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an object detection model represented by orange squares in the scatter plot.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model used in the analysis of the image.

YOLOv8
YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detection model used for analysis in the text.<SEP>An anchor-based real-time object detection model developed by Ultralytics.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an object detection model represented by purple inverted triangles in the scatter plot.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detection model used in the analysis of the image.

TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin
TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin is a specialized NMS implementation that includes multiple kernels for efficient non-maximum suppression.<SEP>TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin is a software component used for efficient Non-Maximum Suppression in object detection.

EfficientNMS
EfficientNMS is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin responsible for non-maximum suppression.

AP
AP refers to Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the accuracy of object detection models.<SEP>AP stands for Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the performance of object detection models.

NMS Execution Time
NMS Execution Time is the time required to perform non-maximum suppression, influenced by the number of boxes and hyperparameters.

End-to-End Speed Benchmark
End-to-End Speed Benchmark is a method for evaluating the total speed of real-time detectors, including all processing steps.<SEP>An evaluation framework designed to compare the end-to-end speed of real-time object detectors, involving benchmark datasets and processing techniques.

EfficientNMSFilter
EfficientNMSFilter is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin used for filtering prediction boxes during non-maximum suppression.<SEP>EfficientNMSFilter is a filtering mechanism used to optimize the Non-Maximum Suppression process.

AP Results
AP Results refer to the Average Precision metrics obtained from evaluating object detection models on benchmark datasets.

RadixSort
RadixSort is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin used for sorting prediction boxes during non-maximum suppression.<SEP>RadixSort is an algorithm used for sorting data efficiently, referenced in the context of NMS execution.

COCO Val2017
A widely used benchmark dataset for object detection tasks, selected for calculating average execution time across multiple images.

End-to-End Speed
End-to-End Speed refers to the total processing time of a detector, including all stages from input to output.<SEP>End-to-End Speed refers to the overall performance of object detection algorithms from input to output.

YOLOv7
An anchor-based real-time object detection model developed by Ultralytics.<SEP>YOLOv7 is an object detection model represented by teal diamonds in the scatter plot.<SEP>YOLOv7 is an anchor-based object detection model mentioned in the analysis.

TensorRT
NVIDIA's platform for deep learning and computer vision, including plugins for optimizing inference.

YOLOv6
An anchor-based real-time object detection model developed by Alibaba.<SEP>YOLOv6 is an anchor-free object detection model mentioned in the analysis.

PP-YOLOE
An anchor-free real-time object detection model developed by PaddlePaddle.<SEP>PP-YOLOE is an object detection model represented by yellow circles in the scatter plot.<SEP>PP-YOLOE is an anchor-free object detection model mentioned in the analysis.

EfficientNMSPlugin
A TensorRT plugin for optimizing Non-Maximum Suppression in object detection pipelines.

I/O
Input/Output operations, excluded from the end-to-end speed calculation.

MemoryCopy
Memory copy operations, excluded from the end-to-end speed calculation.

PaddlePaddle
PaddlePaddle is the organization responsible for developing PP-YOLOE, an anchor-free real-time object detection model.

Ultralytics
Ultralytics is the organization responsible for developing YOLOv5 and YOLOv8, two anchor-based real-time object detection models.

Alibaba
Alibaba is the organization responsible for developing YOLOv6, an anchor-based real-time object detection model.

NVIDIA
NVIDIA is the organization that developed TensorRT, a platform for deep learning and computer vision.

This image presents Figure 1 from the RT-DETR research paper, illustrating a performance benchmark between the proposed RT-DETR model and existing YOLO series detectors (v5 through v8). The scatter plot maps COCO Average Precision (AP) against inference latency on a T4 GPU. It visually substantiates the paper's claim that RT-DETR achieves superior speed-accuracy trade-offs, positioning the red RT-DETR curve above and to the left of competing models, indicating higher accuracy with lower latency.

This image shows page 2 of the research paper introducing RT-DETR, a real-time end-to-end object detector. The text details the proposed architecture's efficiency improvements over existing DETR and YOLO models, specifically addressing computational bottlenecks and query initialization. It also provides a literature review of related real-time and end-to-end detection frameworks.

This image is a page from a technical paper analyzing the end-to-end speed of object detectors, specifically focusing on Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS). It features a bar chart comparing box counts for YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 across different confidence thresholds, and a table detailing the trade-offs between accuracy (AP) and execution time (NMS ms) when adjusting IoU and confidence thresholds. The text explains the experimental setup involving TensorRT and the COCO dataset.

arXiv:2304.08069v3
This is the arXiv identifier for the research paper, indicating its version and publication date.

data.entity_name: DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
data.content:
DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
This is the title of a research paper comparing real-time object detection models, specifically DETRs and YOLOs.

YOLOv6-v3.0
YOLOv6-v3.0 is an object detection model represented by black stars in the scatter plot.

COCO AP
COCO AP is a metric used to measure the accuracy of object detection models, displayed on the Y-axis of the scatter plot.

End-to-end Latency T4 TensorRT FP16
End-to-end Latency T4 TensorRT FP16 is a metric measuring the speed of object detection models, displayed on the X-axis of the scatter plot.

R50 Scaled
R50 Scaled refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

S
S refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

R18 Scaled
R18 Scaled refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

M
M refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

L
L refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

X
X refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

R50
R50 refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

R101
R101 refers to a specific configuration or size of a model, annotated on the scatter plot.

Light Gray Grid
The light gray grid is a background feature of the scatter plot chart.

DETRs
DETRs refers to a class of object detection models based on the Transformer architecture, known for their end-to-end approach.

YOLOs
YOLOs are a family of real-time object detection models known for their speed and efficiency.

RT-DETR-R101
RT-DETR-R101 is a variant of RT-DETR with a ResNet-101 backbone, achieving 54.3% AP and 74 FPS.

RT-DETR-R50
RT-DETR-R50 is a variant of RT-DETR with a ResNet-50 backbone, achieving 53.1% AP and 108 FPS.

Introduction
Introduction is the section of the paper that provides background and motivation for the research.

Related Work
Related Work is the section of the paper that reviews existing research and compares it with the proposed work.

Real-time Object Detectors
Real-time Object Detectors is a subsection of Section 2, focusing on models like YOLO that prioritize speed.

Section 2
Section 2 is the 'Related Work' section of the paper, discussing previous object detection methods.

End-to-end Object Detectors
End-to-end Object Detectors is a subsection of Section 2, discussing Transformer-based models like DETR.

Academic Research Paper
Academic Research Paper is the type of document containing the content analyzed in the image.

Paper
The Paper is titled 'DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection' and presents research on object detection models.

Two-column Layout
Two-column Layout is the formatting style used in the paper, with text divided into two columns.

Black Text
Black Text refers to the color of the text on the page, which is black.<SEP>Black Text refers to the textual content displayed on the white background of the image.

Image
Image is a visual representation of a page from the academic research paper.

Vision Pages
Vision Pages is a directory containing images related to the paper's content.

White Background
White Background refers to the color of the page's background, which is white.<SEP>White Background is the surface on which the text and visuals are displayed in the image.

page_2.png
page_2.png is the filename of the image containing the second page of the paper.

GitHub
GitHub is a platform for hosting and collaborating on software projects.<SEP>GitHub is a platform where the TensorRT plugin is hosted.

lqx94
lqx94 is the username of the individual who uploaded the file to GitHub.

Documents
Documents is a folder on the user's system containing the file.

data
data is a directory containing files related to the project.

knowledge
knowledge is a directory containing knowledge-related files.

Computer Vision
Computer Vision is a field of study focused on enabling computers to interpret and understand visual information from the world.

doc
doc is a directory containing documentation files.

Object Detection Algorithms
Object Detection Algorithms are methods used in computer vision to identify and locate objects within images or videos.

Number of Boxes
Number of Boxes refers to the count of detected objects in an image, influenced by the confidence threshold.

Table 1
Table 1 presents the effect of IoU threshold and confidence threshold on accuracy and NMS execution time.

Bar Chart
Bar Chart is a visual representation of data comparing the number of boxes for different models at varying confidence thresholds.

Section 3
Section 3 discusses the end-to-end speed of object detectors, focusing on NMS analysis and benchmarking.

Anchor-Based Models
Anchor-Based Models are object detection algorithms that use predefined bounding box shapes (anchors) to detect objects.

Anchor-Free Models
Anchor-Free Models are object detection algorithms that do not rely on predefined bounding box shapes.

Page 3
Page 3 is part of a technical research paper discussing computer vision optimization techniques.

T4
T4 is a type of GPU used for testing object detection algorithms.

Technical Research Paper
Technical Research Paper is a document presenting findings on the optimization of object detection algorithms.

Image Path
Image Path is the location of the image file on the computer system.

Document
Document refers to the collection of pages containing the technical research paper.

Two-Column Academic Format
Two-Column Academic Format is the layout used for presenting research in academic journals.

GitHub Repository
GitHub Repository is a source code hosting platform where the TensorRT plugin is available.

Execution Time
NMS execution time depends on the number of boxes and thresholds applied.

Detector Speed
COCO val2017 is used to compare the speed of various object detectors.
